_meta.author: RESOLUTE Citizen autopilot — scorecard-refine-local cron
_meta.note: LOCAL VA Tidewater batch 2026-06-04 17:50 — Suffolk Mayor (Duman) + Vice Mayor (Ward) + Chuckatuck Council (Barlow) + Commonwealth's Attorney (Pleas), plus Virginia Beach D4 Council (Ross-Hammond). rick-west DEFERRED 8th-consecutive (Chesapeake Mayor — unresolved slug collision with OK State Rep Rick West; refine-records.py:366 by_slug dict silently overwrites VA record with OK; prior 7th-defer escalation notified Adam at warning-level — NOT re-escalating to avoid spam per runbook). CROSS-BATCH CORRECTION: prior batch notes (Bennett, Wright) referenced Suffolk Jan 2020 2A 'Constitutional City' resolution as 4-3; three independent researchers (Ward/Barlow/Duman batches) confirm the actual votes were 7-0 Dec 18 2019 (Duman absent on family vacation; Bennett MOTIONED) and 7-0 Jan 15 2020 (Mayor Linda T. Johnson absent; Duman present and YES). No 4-3 Suffolk 2A vote exists in the record. Worth flagging on next QA pass.

profile.background: Mayor of City of Suffolk, Virginia (officially nonpartisan; Independent on filings; 2024 endorsed by Hampton Roads Black Caucus — center-Independent record, not R-aligned). Age 75. Elected Mayor Nov 3, 2020 (defeated incumbent Linda T. Johnson); re-elected Nov 5, 2024 in 3-way at-large race (34.9% Duman vs 34.5% Jenkins vs 30.3% Bosselman — 161-vote margin). Term Jan 2025 - Dec 2028; publicly announced he will NOT seek re-election 2028. Prior service: Suffolk City Council, Chuckatuck Borough, Jan 3, 2011 - Dec 2020 (~10 yrs; re-elected 2014/2018). Suffolk EDA, Western Tidewater Regional Jail Board, Western Tidewater Water Authority. ODU grad; commissioned officer U.S. Army Reserve. Owns Mike Duman Auto Sales / Rental & Collision in Suffolk (founded 1980 — youngest Ford-Mercury dealer in U.S. at 29). Suffolk's 2024 'First Citizen.' Faith: no public profession of Christ; hosts annual civic Leadership Prayer Breakfast (National Day of Prayer) as Mayor — ceremonial.

mike-duman.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] 5+ years as Mayor + 10 years on Council = densest possible local record. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 4T / 3F / 23 NULL = 57.1% — coherent moderate-Independent pragmatist profile, not a Christian-conservative profile. HARD FALSES: (a) signed June 2021 official City of Suffolk Mayor's proclamation declaring 'LGBTQ Pride Month' for inaugural Suffolk Pride at Bennett's Creek Park → biblical_marriage q4 FALSE; (b) backed/oversaw Oct 2021 city employee COVID vaccine-or-testing mandate, drew formal threat-to-sue letter from his own City Prosecutor, did not reverse → refuse_state_overreach q2 FALSE; (c) participated in Mar 29 2022 Hampton Roads Regional Mayors Forum that explicitly framed gun violence as 'public health crisis,' supplied 'learn from successes/failures…be more efficient with resources' quote, did not push back on framing → public_justice q3 FALSE. HARD TRUE: voted YES Jan 15 2020 on Suffolk 'Constitutional City' 2A resolution which named & opposed the then-pending VA red-flag/magazine-limit/AWB/registry package (7-0; Mayor Linda T. Johnson absent that night) AND publicly stated Dec 2019 at SB16 hearing that proposed VA firearms restrictions would make many Suffolk residents (including himself) felons + he would 'not favor the city expending resources to enforce them' → self_defense q1 TRUE HARD. SOFT TRUES: same Dec 2019 statement + Jan 2020 vote map to refuse_state_overreach q0 (asserted local control vs state), q1 (declined state strings), q4 (refused to enforce unconstitutional state directives). SOFT FALSE: Apr-Jun 2026 Suffolk Council moved to BLOCK ICE office siting on Wilroy Road (City Manager Hughes drafted letters to large-industrial owners; Council revised industrial-zoning proffers to prohibit detention uses); Duman did not dissent or break from collective city posture → border_immigration q1 FALSE SOFT. NULL on remaining 23 cells per evidence-first discipline: no profession of Christ (civic prayer-breakfast hosting is ceremonial, not credo); ceremonial swearing-in praise of SPD does NOT meet HIGH BAR for public_justice q0; FY26 budget held rate at $1.07 producing ~4.71% effective tax increase via reassessment with sole NO Tim Johnson pushing 2¢ cut, FY27 unanimous 1¢ cut → economic_stewardship q2 mixed-signal NULL per discipline (held rate is not a hike but is not a cut either; Bennett & Johnson pushed harder for restraint). Suffolk NOT on May 30 2025 DHS sanctuary list (Portsmouth was); no formal sanctuary ordinance → border_immigration q2 NULL. Recommend revisiting after any 2026 Pride/sanctuary/ICE/2A/abortion action by Council.

set.office: Vice Mayor / City Council - Nansemond Borough

profile.background: Suffolk City Council (officially nonpartisan; ran as Independent on 2012 ballot), Nansemond Borough since Jan 2, 2013 (4 consecutive terms — repeatedly unopposed; Nov 2024 won ~98% over write-ins). Vice Mayor since Jan 3, 2023 (elected by colleagues). Term ends Dec 31, 2028; NOT on Nov 2026 ballot. Founder/publisher Harambee Communications: Suffolk Happenings newspaper, Hampton Roads Happenings newspaper, Having Church Magazine (the magazine title is a Black-church cultural marker but not a personal profession). Hosts 30-min TV and radio show. Former president Nansemond-Suffolk NAACP branch. Sits on Western Tidewater Regional Jail Authority Board + City/School Advisory Committee on Collaborative Fiscal Concerns. Suffolk Democratic Committee does NOT list him.

lue-ward.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] 13-year incumbent Vice Mayor; consistently unopposed → light public-record profile. Single-cell sweep located only 1 hard rubric data point: voted YES on Jan 15 2020 Suffolk 'Constitutional City' 2A resolution (7-0; Mayor Linda T. Johnson absent), which named and opposed the then-pending Virginia red-flag/magazine-limit/AWB/registry package → self_defense q1 TRUE (HARD by virtue of the vote). NULL on refuse_state_overreach cells — unlike Duman, Ward did not separately quote-bear that he would refuse to enforce the state package; the vote alone is too thin to score 5 cells. FY26 budget: voted YES on 7-1 hold of $1.07 rate (sole NO Tim Johnson sought 2¢ cut); FY27 unanimous 8-0 1¢ cut — held-rate position is not a hike per discipline → economic_stewardship q2 NULL. Publishes Having Church Magazine via Harambee — Black-church cultural marker, NOT personal credo → christian_liberty q0 NULL. No social media handles, campaign Facebook, or VPAP record surfaced (404 on VPAP). Confidence held at roster_verified — only 1 of 30 cells scored, even after thorough sweep documenting the absence (this is honest sparseness, not lazy research). Recommend revisiting only on a documented new vote (Pride/sanctuary/abortion/firearms/CRT-SOGI) or a campaign-website launch ahead of 2028 re-election.

profile.background: Suffolk Commonwealth's Attorney (D). Re-elected Nov 4, 2025 in rematch over Indep defense-attorney Patrick Bales 60%/39% (vs. 51.9%/47.5% in 2021). New 4-year term Jan 2026 - Dec 2029. First Black female Suffolk CA. Career prosecutor ~16 yrs Deputy CA in Norfolk + Suffolk + Asst CA in Sussex + Emporia/Greensville before 2021 election; succeeded Phil Ferguson (44 yrs in office). JD Indiana University; BA Agnes Scott College; Green Run HS (Virginia Beach) + Governor's School for the Arts, Norfolk. Volunteer Western Tidewater Medical Reserve Corps + VA Dental Mission of Mercy. Faith not publicly documented.

narendra-pleas.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] HETERODOX-D CATCH. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 2T / 1F / 27 NULL = 66.7% (small denominator). HARD TRUES (both prosecutor-central cells): (a) public_justice q0 — Operation Ceasefire (Apr 2023, $326K state grant funding full-time prosecutor + legal assistant dedicated to violent-crime focused-deterrence with SPD); joint press conferences with Chief Al Chandler and ATF; affirmative pro-LE prosecutor work, well beyond routine governance; (b) public_justice q1 — explicit on-record rejection of 'progressive prosecutor' label by name to Suffolk News-Herald Feb 2022: 'I'm not a progressive prosecutor. Please don't label me as such.' No Color of Change / Justice & Public Safety PAC / Soros-network endorsement located. SOFT FALSE: public_justice q3 — frames violent-crime causes as 'lack of education, lack of resources'; runs Behavioral Health Docket diverting mental-health-linked cases from jail to services (mainstream-D diversionist language); does NOT cleanly meet 'refused public-health excuse' criterion. NULL on remaining 27 cells per evidence-first discipline: zero public statement located on abortion enforcement (critical gap for a prosecutor), marriage, trans/SOGI, parental rights, ICE detainer policy, faith profession. Western Tidewater Regional Jail historically holds ICE detainees (jail-authority policy, NOT the CA's; no Pleas position located). Don't infer from D party — her own rejection of the 'progressive prosecutor' label is the dominant signal. Defeated Bales by 21 points in 2025 rematch (up from 4-point 2021 win) — local electorate validated the first-term record. Recommend revisiting after any decline-to-prosecute / abortion-enforcement / ICE-cooperation statement; next election Nov 2029.

profile.background: Suffolk City Council, Chuckatuck Borough (officially nonpartisan). Appointed Feb 3, 2021 to fill vacancy; elected Nov 8, 2022 (44.5%, 3-way race over Anderson 29.6% and Bales 24.5%); sworn in to full 4-year term Jan 3, 2023. Term ends Dec 31, 2026 — UP IN NOV 2026. Co-owns Cotton Plains Farm in Chuckatuck with husband Joseph Barlow Jr (multigenerational cotton/soy/corn/wheat/vegetables). Western Branch HS '78; BS Agriculture Virginia Tech '82; nearly 40 yrs in agriculture. 10 yrs VDACS Board (4 as president); 4 yrs VT Board of Visitors; 2016 VALOR Fellow; Cotton Board Research & Promotion Program; Suffolk USDA FSA county committee chair; VA Farm Bureau AgPac Board + Cotton Advisory Board; 2010 VA Farm Bureau 'Farm Woman of the Year.' Active member Wesley Chapel United Methodist Church in Chuckatuck (UMC, not GMC-disaffiliated).

shelley-butler-barlow.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] Dense local record for a 5-year incumbent. 30 LOCAL cells scored as 4T / 2F / 24 NULL = 66.7%. HARD TRUES: christian_liberty q0 + q3 — personally delivered the Invocation at Council on Apr 2 2025 and Sept 4 2024 (per Suffolk Council minutes); active Wesley Chapel UMC member listed on official city bio. Invocation delivery is a public Christian act in official capacity (q3 HARD); the invocation + UMC listing together meet the 'publicly profess Christ in public office' rubric (q0 HARD-leaning). SOFT TRUE: public_justice q0 — Oct 7 2025 after Chief Buie report she called speed-camera enforcement trends 'encouraging,' asked how many more cameras could be deployed, and voiced support for EXPANDING police presence — affirmative advocacy beyond routine governance (HIGH BAR met SOFT); refuse_state_overreach q0 — Mar 2026 federal-detention warehouse discussion she explicitly asserted limits of municipal authority vs federal preemption ('What we've done is about the limit of what we can do') + Dec 2025 pushed for local-option referendum authority from Richmond (local control of local revenue). HARD FALSE: border_immigration q2 — joined unanimous Mar 2026 Suffolk Council push to discourage federal detention centers in 100,000+ sq ft warehouses (letters to property owners, industrial-zoning proffer revision); her own quotes: 'What I would like is for government not to have human beings stacked up in warehouses waiting to be deported. It's a sickening thing to think about'; 'human beings being housed in warehouse facilities — I just don't think anybody thinks that's a great idea' — functionally a soft-sanctuary posture against ICE infrastructure. SOFT FALSE: economic_stewardship q2 — publicly floated a 1% local-option education tax voter referendum at Dec 2025 work session ('Does more money always fix everything? No, but it would certainly help') — PRO-TAX-INCREASE posture (counterbalanced by FY26 budget motion-second to hold rate $1.07 and FY27 unanimous 1¢ cut). NULL on self_defense q1 — NOT seated for Jan 2020 2A vote (appointed Feb 2021); no subsequent firearms ordinance position. NULL on biblical_marriage / family_child_sovereignty / election_integrity / sanctity_of_life — no recorded position. NOTE: Wesley Chapel UMC remains active United Methodist (not GMC-disaffiliated) post-2024 General Conference rollback of bans on same-sex marriage and LGBTQ clergy — denominational context is worth flagging but she's made NO public statement endorsing or opposing the UMC's 2024 changes (silent → no q0 demerit). Cotton Plains conflict-discipline: Feb 2022 properly abstained on city lease involving her family farm. Candidacy: term ends Dec 2026 — set general_candidate (Nov 2026 ballot in ~5 months; assumed re-running absent retirement statement). Recommend revisiting after Nov 2026 election + any future Council vote on Pride / abortion / firearms / CRT-SOGI.

profile.background: Virginia Beach City Council, District 4 (officially nonpartisan; D-aligned per Progressive Voters Guide 2022 listing). Won newly-created D4 seat Nov 8 2022 unopposed under post-VRA 10-district system; sworn in Jan 1 2023. Term ends Dec 31 2026 — UP IN NOV 2026. Prior service: Kempsville District 2013-2017 (won 2012, LOST 2016 reelection to Jessica Abbott 58.9%/40.5%). Distinguished Professor Emerita of Music & Humanities + former Director of Service-Learning & Civic Engagement at Norfolk State University; 2009 NSU Distinguished Faculty Award. BA Ithaca College; MM College of New Jersey; PhD Education Leadership U Denver. FOUNDER & Chairman of Virginia African American Cultural Center (VAACC) — VBCC unanimously gifted ~4.83 acres to establish the center. CURRENTLY chairs the Mayor's IDEA Commission (Inclusion/Diversity/Equity/Accessibility). Faith not publicly documented. Was NOT on Council during May 2019 mass shooting or Jan 2020 2A Constitutional City vote (lost 2016, returned 2023).

amelia-ross-hammond.notes_set: [2026-06-04 17:50 RESOLUTE refinement] 30 LOCAL cells scored as 0T / 1F / 29 NULL = 0% (sparse-data signal). Single HARD CELL: economic_stewardship q2 FALSE — voted YES on every budget of her current tenure (FY24 8-3, FY25 10-1, FY26 10-1 with MEALS-TAX hike, FY27 11-0 with no rate cut despite ~6% assessment rise → effective tax increase). Multiple SOFT D-aligned signals: Progressive Voters Guide 2022 listing; chairs Mayor's IDEA Commission (DEI framework); founded Virginia African American Cultural Center; participated in LWV Juneteenth event — none meet the rubric's TRUE/FALSE evidentiary bar individually (per rules, party-vibe signals are NULL, not FALSE). VAACC land gift was UNANIMOUS Council vote — self-interested project for her own nonprofit; flag for ethics/conflict context, not a rubric cell. Virginia Beach scored a perfect Municipal Equality Index 6 consecutive years (per Visit VB) — but no specific Council vote tied to Ross-Hammond on a Pride proclamation. VB was on the May 30 2025 DHS sanctuary list but pushed back as 'erroneous' (Sheriff's Office cooperates with ICE — 78 inmates transferred per City's response — but Sheriff is SEPARATE elected office, not Council). Modest developer donations ($2,000) per Princess Anne Indy reporting. Up for Nov 2026 reelection (5 months) — candidacy_status: general_candidate (assumed re-running absent retirement statement). Confidence evidence_local on strength of the budget-vote roll calls and the exhaustive search documenting absence elsewhere — but be aware this is honest sparseness, not lazy research.
